title: AgentLedger Agent Outcome Graph
description: Formal contract for the Agent Outcome Graph (CLAUDE.md Phase 3): the moat that makes cost -> agent -> outcome -> value queryable with an attribution confidence on every edge. Nodes are identities (human AND non-human/agent), agent runs, and business outcomes; edges connect them and ALWAYS carry an attribution_confidence in [0,1] (1.0 for deterministic/agent-stamped links, <1.0 for probabilistic links from the attribution matcher). This document is the abstract serialization; its physical realization is the Postgres + ClickHouse tables/views described in README.md (identities/agents -> v_identities, agent_runs, outcomes, v_outcome_graph). VERSION 1.1.0 (additive, non-breaking — ADR-040): the `produced` edge may now also carry the attribution engine v2 enrichment (attribution_method, confidence_calibrated, signal_contributions, counterfactual_delta, coalition_id, shapley_value/cost, model_version), realized in Postgres attribution_edges. attribution_confidence remains required and equals confidence_calibrated of the winning edge. SECURITY: nodes carry identifiers and categorical/financial attributes only — never raw prompt/completion content (CLAUDE.md rule 2); signal_contributions hold evidence REFERENCES (PR URL, ticket id, timestamps, overlap %), never copied content (§7).

tenant_id.description: Tenant that owns this subgraph; every node and edge is scoped to it.

confidence.description: Attribution confidence in [0,1]. 1.0 == deterministic (SDK/agent-stamped); <1.0 == probabilistic (attribution matcher, time/identity/token signals).

signalContribution.description: One signal's contribution to a probabilistic edge's score — the explanation that makes the confidence auditable (attribution engine v2, sub-phase 3.3).

signal.description: Signal name, e.g. temporal_proximity | identity_match | artifact_overlap | content_match | behavioral.

weighted_log_odds.description: signal_weight x value — this signal's additive contribution to the log-odds.

evidence_ref.description: Reference to the raw evidence (PR URL, ticket id, timestamp, file-overlap %) — NEVER copied prompt/completion content (CLAUDE.md rule 2; §7).

identity_type.description: First-class non-human identities (agents) sit alongside humans — CLAUDE.md Phase 3.

owner_user_id.description: Agents only: the human identity that owns/operates this NHI.

total_cost_usd.description: Fully-observed AI cost of the run (sum of its llm_calls).

edge_type.description: performed_by: agent_run -> identity(human). operates: identity(human) -> identity(agent NHI). incurred_cost: agent_run -> cost (deterministic). produced: agent_run -> outcome (carries the matcher's confidence). valued_at: outcome -> business value (deterministic).

deterministic.description: True when the link is asserted (agent-stamped/SDK/structural), implying attribution_confidence == 1.0.

attribution_method.description: v1.1 — how the `produced` edge was scored. deterministic: ground-truth link (trailer/session-id/API-close). probabilistic: log-linear scorer over signals. shapley: a coalition member's marginal-contribution allocation.

confidence_calibrated.description: v1.1 — post-calibration confidence (isotonic/Platt). Equals attribution_confidence; the calibrated number a 0.8 of which means ~80% precision.

confidence_raw.description: v1.1 — pre-calibration model output, kept for backtesting.

signal_contributions.description: v1.1 — the per-signal breakdown behind a probabilistic confidence. Empty for deterministic edges.

counterfactual_delta.description: v1.1 — incremental fraction of the outcome above the identity/team baseline (sub-phase 3.4); value_attributed is scaled by this, not the gross.

coalition_id.description: v1.1 — set when this edge is one member of a multi-agent coalition (sub-phase 3.5).

shapley_value.description: v1.1 — this member's Shapley-allocated share of the outcome value (coalition edges only).

shapley_cost.description: v1.1 — this member's Shapley-allocated share of the cost (coalition edges only).

model_version.description: v1.1 — attribution_model_versions.version that produced this edge, so any historical score is reproducible (CLAUDE.md rule 10).
